title: calendula arvensis subsp. hydruntina
intro: Calendula arvensis subsp. hydruntina is a lesser-known subspecies of the common marigold plant, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae, and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Calendula arvensis subsp. hydruntina include its ability to accelerate wound healing, soothe skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and reduce inflammation and pain. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion, ointment, or tincture, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally, under the guidance of a healthcare professional, to harness its medicinal properties.
